title: cluster_signs_by_embedding data payload
description: v0.28.0 sign-class clustering. Runs k-means++ (Lloyd's algorithm, deterministic seed mulberry32(20260525)) over the L2-normalized 100-dim sign2vec embeddings produced by v0.23. Surfaces empirical sign-semantic groups (numerals, common syllabograms, compound logograms, phonetic-variant families) without scholar curation. Companion to find_similar_signs: that tool answers `who are this sign's nearest neighbors?`, this tool answers `does the embedding space separate into coherent CLASSES, and what are they?`. The per-cluster member dumps + representative-sign tables are designed to be a publishable empirical sign-taxonomy in their own right. Wrapped in StructuredEnvelope.

k.description: Effective number of clusters produced. May be lower than the request if fewer than k signs are available, or if the requested k was outside [2, 50] (in which case it is clamped and a warning is emitted).

total_signs_clustered.description: How many signs from the sign2vec vocab were partitioned. Equals total_signs_indexed when the index is healthy.

iterations_run.description: Lloyd's-algorithm iterations actually executed. Equals max_iterations when convergence wasn't reached.

converged.description: True iff no assignment changed in the final pass (the standard k-means stopping criterion).

silhouette_score.description: Mean silhouette coefficient over all clustered signs. Range [-1, 1]; > 0 indicates the clustering produces real separation (each point closer to its own cluster than to its nearest neighbor cluster). v0.28 calibration floor is > 0.05.

clusters.description: Per-cluster reports, sorted by `size` descending. The 0-indexed `id` field preserves the original cluster identity, which is reproducible across runs given the deterministic seed.

id.description: 0-indexed cluster identifier. Reproducible across runs at the same k thanks to the fixed mulberry32 seed.

size.description: Number of sign vocab members assigned to this cluster.

mean_intra_cluster_cosine.description: Average pairwise cosine similarity over all member-pairs in this cluster. Higher = tighter cluster. For singletons, 1.0 by convention.

nearest_other_cluster_distance.description: Euclidean distance from this cluster's centroid to the nearest other centroid. Larger = better separation from the rest of the partition.

representative_signs.description: Top 3 cluster members ranked by cosine to the cluster centroid — the signs that most exemplify the cluster's center.

top_signs_by_occurrence.description: Top 10 cluster members by corpus occurrence count. Where representative_signs surfaces the geometric center, this surfaces the diagnostic high-frequency members (useful for label inference and human inspection).

all_members.description: Full member list, sorted by occurrence count desc.

suggested_label.description: Best-effort human label derived from the top-10 members by a documented surface-form heuristic: ≥60% pure digit strings → 'numerical'; ≥60% containing × (e.g. LAGAB×HAL) or compound markers → 'compound_logogram'; ≥60% ABZNNN-prefixed → 'abz_syllabogram_family'; ≥60% with subscript-digit suffix → 'phonetic_variant_family'; else 'mixed_distributional_group_<id>'. Treat as literature-facing shorthand, not a typological claim.

total_signs_indexed.description: Vocab size of the underlying sign2vec index — signs occurring ≥ min_occurrences times in the corpus.

embedding_dim.description: Truncated-SVD rank K of the underlying embeddings (default 100 in v0.23).

window_size.description: ±window context width used at embedding-build time.

build_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the sign-embeddings index was produced. Empty string when the index is unavailable.

sign.description: Sign code (typically an ABZ identifier, a digit string, or a compound logogram name).

occurrences.description: Total occurrences of this sign in the cleaned corpus.

cosine_to_centroid.description: Cosine similarity between this sign's embedding and its cluster centroid. Higher = more representative of the cluster's center.
